_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: Seven proud old currencies gave up their names so she could be born. She has never forgotten the debt.

museum_objects.title: Maastricht Treaty Signing Photograph
museum_objects.description: Official EU photograph of the signing of the Maastricht Treaty, which laid the legal foundation for the euro.
museum_objects.significance: Sofia's entire existence was negotiated into being in this room — every clause in this treaty is a small piece of national sovereignty someone agreed to give up for her.
museum_objects.plain_language: An official photo of the 1992 treaty signing that legally created the plan for a shared European currency.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

display_alternatives.title: The Treaty of Maastricht on display in Rome, 1997 (clip 3).jpg

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so. | IMAGE QA FAIL: The image does not show a photograph of people signing a treaty, it shows a document or book being held, which does not match the claim of a 'Maastricht Treaty Signing Photograph'.

museum_objects.title: Euro Banknote Design ('Ages and Styles of Europe')
museum_objects.description: Robert Kalina's winning design for euro banknotes, depicting generic architectural styles rather than real, nationally-specific landmarks.
museum_objects.significance: Sofia couldn't show a single real building from a single real country without offending eleven others — so she was designed to look like everywhere and nowhere at once.
museum_objects.plain_language: The winning design for euro banknotes, deliberately showing fake generic buildings instead of real ones from any single country, to avoid favoritism.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Euro Coin Design by Luc Luycx
museum_objects.description: The winning common-side design for euro coins, created by Belgian designer Luc Luycx at the Royal Belgian Mint.
museum_objects.significance: One side of every euro coin says something different depending which country made it — Sofia's other side had to say the exact same thing everywhere.
museum_objects.plain_language: The design used on one side of every euro coin, made the same across all countries, while the other side varies by nation.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: ECB Duisenberg Farewell Speech Records
museum_objects.description: The official ECB speech marking the final retirement of the seven founding legacy currencies' legal tender status.
museum_objects.significance: This speech is the funeral for seven currencies at once — the deutsche mark, franc, lira, peseta, escudo, drachma, and guilder all officially died the same week Sofia became the only option left.
museum_objects.plain_language: An official 2002 speech marking the exact moment the old European currencies — like the franc, lira, and deutsche mark — stopped being legal money for good.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Syntagma Square Protest Photographs (2011)
museum_objects.description: Photographs documenting mass protests in Athens against austerity measures during the Greek debt crisis.
museum_objects.significance: Sofia's unity was tested hardest here — a country furious that sharing her meant losing control of its own economic destiny.
museum_objects.plain_language: Photos of massive 2011 protests in Athens, Greece, against harsh economic measures tied to using the shared euro currency during a debt crisis.
museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so.

primary_sources.description: Transcript of the pivotal ECB speech during the eurozone debt crisis, credited with stabilizing the euro.
primary_sources.plain_language: The transcript of a famous 2012 speech where the ECB president promised to do whatever was needed to save the euro.

world_map.map_description: Sofia was born from the Maastricht Treaty and became the shared currency of most of Europe, tested by financial crises from Athens to Frankfurt.

nodes.label: Maastricht, Netherlands
nodes.description: Where the 1992 treaty establishing the framework for European Monetary Union was signed.

nodes.label: Frankfurt (ECB HQ)
nodes.description: Headquarters of the European Central Bank, which manages the euro across the eurozone.

nodes.label: Brussels (Cinquantenaire)
nodes.description: Home to EU institutions and the Royal Belgian Mint, which designed the common euro coin side.

nodes.label: Athens (Syntagma Square)
nodes.description: Site of major protests during the Greek debt crisis, one of the euro's most severe internal tests.

nodes.label: Rome
nodes.description: Capital of one of the seven founding countries whose legacy currency, the lira, was retired for the euro.

historical_map_source.title: ECB — How the Euro Became Our Money
